content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN books in the library where the number of pages in the ithi^{th}ith book is BiB_iBi​. These numbers, B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots,B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, are given to you. Chef will allocate all these NNN books to AAA students in contiguous order, which means that the ithi^{th}ith student will be allocated a contiguous set of books (ie. Bj,Bj+1,…,Bj+kB_j, B_{j+1}, \dots,B_{j+k}Bj​,Bj+1​,…,Bj+k​, for some jjj and kkk). Every student should be allocated at least one book. **Minimize** the **maximum** sum of pages allocated to a student. Find that **minimum** value.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN, AAA — the number books and students.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1,B_2,\ldots,B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and A — the number of books and the number of students. The second line contains N space‑separated integers B1, B2, …, BN, where Bi is the number of pages in the i‑th book.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible value of the maximum number of pages allocated to any student.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: optimal split is [4,8] and [2,6,10] giving max sum 16. Test case 2: each student gets one book, max pages = 18.
Best split is [10,20] and [30]; the larger sum is 30, which is minimal.

solution.approach: The answer lies between max(Bi) (no student can receive less than the largest single book) and sum(Bi) (one student receives all books). Perform binary search on this range. For a candidate value X, greedily allocate books to the current student until adding the next book would exceed X, then move to the next student. If we need more than A students, X is too small; otherwise X is feasible. The smallest feasible X is the required answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log S) per test case, where S = sum of pages. The total over all test cases is O((total N)·log S).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the input array.
